rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF NANCY CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/23/2020: The following issues must be addressed: 1) The small estate affidavits signed by the 4 distributees have the following defects: A) No certified copy of the decedent's death certificate was attached. See Probate Code section 13101(d) & LRSF 14.93(E)(2). B) Paragraph 9 of each affidavit needs to state that no other person has a "superior" right. See section 13101(a)(9). C) The notary acknowledgement certificates included in the affidavits of Jacqueline Chan & Bruce Chan are defective in that they did not provide for a boxed notice required by Civil Code section 1189(a)(1). 2) The first sentence of paragraph 17 of the account and report refers to the "first" account and report. Did the petitioner mean to state the "second" account and report? 3) The "filing" date of the investigator's report set forth in paragraph 19 of the account and report is incorrect. 4) Paragraph 19 of the account and report needs to address the issue of whether the estate can bear the cost of the deferred assessment. See LRSF 14.78(N). 5) Paragraph 24 of the account and report cites an incorrect local rule number. 6) Paragraph 3 of the declaration of the petitioner's attorney includes services related to the assessment fees alleged to have been provided during the second and final account period, but the proof of payment and request to defer assessment fees were both filed during the first account period. 7) A schedule of liabilities (section 1063(g)) must be provided. 8) A statement of compensation required by section 1064(a)(3) must be provided.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RENKO MATSUDAIRA REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/23/2020: The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 5a & Schedule A-1: Why did the annuity payments continue after the beneficiary's death? 2) Paragraph 9 of the petition: Is the alleged date of execution of the trust accurate? 3) Paragraph 10: How did the petitioner determine that $20,000 would be a reasonable amount of the reserve? 4) The summary of account must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form. 5) Schedule C: Why did the petitioner continue to pay the health insurance premiums after the beneficiary's death? 6) The petitioner's invoice refers to "tangible personal items shipped to Japan", "pickup remaining personal items", and "Ezer Pengo 1000 Banknote". Are they assets of the trust? 7) The money market account balance far exceeds the FDIC limits.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) failure to file a petition for final distribution or approval of a status of report of administration; and 2) failure to file an inventory and appraisal. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARK WILLIAM OLSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 9/23/20. when opening a probate estate, the notice of hearing form required is DE-157. new notice must be mailed on that form for the continued hearing date. in addition, no proposed order has been delivered to Room 202 as required by local rules, and the statement in the declaration that petitioner has obtained bond is insufficient as it fails to list the anticipated liabilities of the decedent, which is required so the court can determine whether bond is sufficient. all documents and proof of service to be filed by noon on 9/10/20. file stamped hard copies and the proposed order to be delivered to room 202 by noon on 9/16/20.
